commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=caa3808338d1e2c75ae9022c505c29a0adba72fb)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a default-disabled structured Doctor health check for stale session locks, dry-run repair effects, and health-check default-selection support in the lint runner.

PR surface: Source +106, Tests +197. Total +303 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blocker: source inspection shows `HealthCheck` is exported through `src/plugin-sdk/health.ts`, and the PR adds `defaultEnabled` to that interface. The requested structured session-lock finding is a new Doctor capability rather than a current-main bug repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer judgment on a protected draft PR's public health-check API boundary plus current-head proof, not a narrow safe repair job.

target.title: Doctor: expose session lock findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes stale session write locks as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for stale lock removal.
- Keeps the existing session-lock note/repair function authoritative for real normal `doctor` / `doctor --fix` behavior.
- Wires the structured check through the existing ordered doctor contribution path.
- Aligns structured repair previews with the real cleanup gate so stale-but-mtime-gated malformed locks are preserved until the cleanup grace period.
- Uses a report-only fix hint for live OpenClaw-owned stale locks so `doctor --lint` does not imply that `doctor --fix` will remove locks that cleanup intentionally preserves.

This is intentionally the session-lock slice from the doctor repair stack. It does not change session directory discovery, lock ownership detection, or the real mutating cleanup path.

## Stack

Stacked on #86627. Current branch head is signed commit `caa3808338d1e2c75ae9022c505c29a0adba72fb` (`fix(doctor): preserve defaultEnabled through health-check normalization`).

## Validation

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts --reporter=dot` (9 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot` (doctor-session-locks 9 passed; doctor-core-checks 14 passed; session-write-lock 50 passed / 3 failed on Windows, see note below)
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/session-write-lock.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/session-write-lock.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/flows/health-check-adapter.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/flows/health-check-adapter.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Validation note:
On Windows, `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts` still fails the same three retry-during-diagnostics tests when run by itself, before or independent of this two-file hint patch:
`retries when a stale lock report disappears before diagnostics`, `retries when a stale lock report is replaced before diagnostics`, and `retries when a stale lock report is replaced by a fresh payload-less lock`. The changed `doctor-session-locks` shard passes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Stale session lock files are reported through `doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks`, and structured repair previews now match the real cleanup gate. Report-only live OpenClaw-owned stale locks are preserved in previews and now receive a report-only operator hint instead of a cleanup-grace retry hint. Fresh malformed stale locks such as `{}` with `missing-pid` / `invalid-createdAt` are also preserved until the mtime cleanup grace period, instead of being previewed as immediately removable.

Real environment tested:
Windows 11 PowerShell, source checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-pr-84366` at signed head `1eaff161f1efa87847a9d1ec3e17b8c3fc489dba`. The CLI proof used an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, an isolated home directory, and a hidden live Node owner process with argv containing `openclaw.mjs` so the real process-argv reader classified the synthetic stale lock as OpenClaw-owned.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created an isolated state dir with `state/agents/main/sessions/report-only.jsonl.lock`.
2. Wrote isolated config `{"session":{"writeLock":{"staleMs":30000}}}`.
3. Started a hidden live owner process with argv containing `openclaw.mjs`.
4. Wrote the lock payload with the live owner pid and a `createdAt` timestamp two minutes in the past.
5. Ran `node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks` with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointing at the isolated state dir.
6. Verified the lock file still existed after the command.
7. Ran the focused doctor/session-lock tests, oxlint, oxfmt, `pnpm tsgo:core`, and `git diff --check` listed above.

Evidence after fix:
Redacted terminal output from the real CLI command path:

```text
{"ok":false,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":22,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/session-locks","severity":"warning","message":"Stale session lock file: <temp>\\state\\agents\\main\\sessions\\report-only.jsonl.lock (too-old)","path":"<temp>\\state\\agents\\main\\sessions\\report-only.jsonl.lock","fixHint":"OpenClaw is preserving this live owned lock; inspect the owning process if it appears stuck."}]}
EXIT_CODE=1
LOCK_EXISTS=true
OWNER_PID=<live-node-owner-pid>
```

Supporting regression output:

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 C:/tmp/openclaw-pr-84366

.........

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)
```

The earlier fresh malformed lock case remains covered by the focused shard: a `{}` lock is detected with `missing-pid` / `invalid-createdAt`, remains non-removable, keeps the cleanup-grace hint, and previews as `would-preserve-mtime-gated-stale-session-lock`.

Observed result after fix:
Structured doctor output no longer over-promises that `doctor --fix` will remove stale locks that the cleanup path intentionally preserves. Preview effects now distinguish removable stale locks, report-only stale locks, and mtime-gated stale malformed locks, and the report-only finding now tells operators to inspect the live owner process if it appears stuck.

What was not tested:
No real long-running OpenClaw agent process was held open; the CLI proof used a live process whose argv is classified by the real OpenClaw owner-process reader. The broader Windows `session-write-lock.test.ts` shard still has the three retry-during-diagnostics failures noted above; this patch does not touch that file or retry path.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
